Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that filters out specific cases from an event_log table The cases to be excluded are those that contain a specific sequence of three activities in direct succession Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order AND where the time difference between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days The final output should be all events from cases that do not meet these criteria

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Key Requirements Identify cases with the exact sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order direct succession no other events in between Check time difference timestamp Confirm Order minus timestamp Create Order more than 5 days Exclude these cases Return all events from remaining cases Ensure proper grouping ordering filtering

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: DuckDB Specifics DuckDB supports window functions LAG LEAD ROW_NUMBER etc DuckDB supports date time arithmetic INTERVAL 5 DAY AGE or direct subtraction DuckDB supports QUALIFY clause WITH EXCEPT NOT IN NOT EXISTS etc Need to handle direct succession carefully This means for a given case when events are ordered by timestamp the activity immediately following Approve Purchase Requisition must be Create Order and the one immediately following that must be Confirm Order

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 1 Identify the sequence and time condition I need to find case_ids that match the criteria I can use window functions to look at the next two activities for each event Lets use LEAD activity 1 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next_activity LEAD activity 2 OVER as next_next_activity LEAD timestamp 1 OVER as next_ts LEAD timestamp 2 OVER as next_next_ts Now filter for the sequence activity Approve Purchase Requisition next_activity Create Order next_next_activity Confirm Order And time condition next_next_ts - next_ts > INTERVAL 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 2 Exclude these cases and return all other events SELECT FROM event_log WHERE case_id NOT IN SELECT case_id FROM MatchedCases ORDER BY case_id timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Step 3 Refine and Check Edge Cases What if there are ties in timestamps assume standard ORDER BY timestamp is sufficient DuckDBs INTERVAL syntax INTERVAL 5 days works LEAD with offset 2 is fine NOT IN is fine What if the sequence appears multiple times DISTINCT case_id handles this direct succession with no other event in between LEAD captures this

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: SelfCorrectionVerification during thought Is LEAD safe for direct succession Yes because LEAD activity 1 gets the very next row What about ties in timestamp stick to ORDER BY timestamp DuckDB interval comparison works Alternatively DATEDIFF day create_order_ts confirm_order_ts > 5 but stick to interval comparison NOT IN vs NOT EXISTS NOT IN fine EXCEPT alternative but stick MATCH_RECOGNIZE alternative but window functions simpler optimal

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check of the query DuckDB syntax WINDOW LEAD INTERVAL NOT IN ORDER BY valid LEAD might return NULL but WHERE clause handles naturally All constraints met query looks solid correct robust
